system.notes: 0kg Undead, urban flyer, #App: 1, Treasure: Small. Special attack; armor 0; flying speed 10m/action; Morale +4. Ghosts are incorporeal spirits of the dead. They often haunt the places related to their death, such as a cemetery, gallows, battlefield, or an abandoned location. Undead: immune to poison, disease, and suffocation. Incorporeal: immune to non-silver, non-magical weapons. Immune to mind-affecting magic. The ghost may possess a target. The target must throw INT 8+ or be possessed and controlled by the ghost. The target may attempt another INT 8+ throw once a week to end the possession. The Exorcism spell will remove the ghost immediately and force it to leave the victim's body. A ghost may only possess one victim at a time. Ghosts always haunt for a reason: be it murder, unjust execution, or improper burial. Without remedying this reason, a defeated ghost will reappear within 3d6 nights. Exorcism, however, will permanently remove a ghost from its haunt and sent its spirit straight to Hell.
